title: Global SSA Market to Reach $61B as Governments Prioritize Space Security, Resilience, and Orbital Safety
summary: The global Space Situational Awareness (SSA) market is projected to reach $61 billion as governments focus on space security, resilience, and orbital safety. Novaspace's latest SSA report highlights a pivotal decade ahead for the industry.

title: The importance of determining an equilibrium state for space traffic management
summary: The rapid expansion of human and robotic activity in low Earth orbit highlights the critical need for effective space traffic management (STM) to maintain safety and sustainability. Establishing an equilibrium state for STM is essential to address growing congestion and collision risks.

title: UK explores Vast space station mission for astronaut with physical disability
summary: The UK government is considering sending astronaut John McFall to Vast's Haven-1 space station, potentially making him the first person with a physical disability to live in orbit. This mission highlights efforts toward inclusivity in space exploration.

title: Impulse Space raises $500 million
summary: Impulse Space has secured $500 million in funding to scale up production of orbital transfer vehicles and other spacecraft. The capital will help meet increasing commercial and government demand for its services.

title: Voyager to acquire lunar lander developer Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Technologies will acquire Astrobotic, a lunar lander and suborbital vehicle developer, in a deal potentially worth $300 million.

title: Researchers call for regulations to protect low Earth orbit environment
summary: Researchers are urging for regulations to safeguard the low Earth orbit environment from the harmful effects of exotic materials released during satellite and space hardware re-entries. The call highlights the growing concern over atmospheric pollution caused by space debris and re-entry events.

title: NASA abandons ‘core module’ concept for commercial space station development
summary: NASA is abandoning its 'core module' concept for commercial space station development due to criticism from involved companies. This marks a shift in strategy for transitioning from the International Space Station to commercial alternatives.

title: France to fly two astronauts on Vast missions
summary: Vast has partnered with the French government to send two French astronauts on its missions, including the inaugural flight to its Haven-1 space station.

title: Northrop Grumman partners with Apex on space-based interceptors for Golden Dome
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex, a Los Angeles-based startup, to develop space-based interceptors for the Golden Dome program. Apex specializes in producing standardized and cost-effective satellite buses.

title: Spaceport facility bonds are now law – and they fundamentally change space infrastructure finance
summary: Spaceport facility bonds have been signed into law, marking a significant shift in how space infrastructure is financed. This legislation is expected to accelerate the development of spaceport facilities across the U.S.

title: 'Hot Jupiter' winds blasting at over 15,000 mph reveal 1st evidence of exoplanets with magnetic fields
summary: Astronomers found the first evidence of magnetic fields on exoplanets by studying high-speed winds over 15,000 mph on 'Hot Jupiters', marking a significant advancement in exoplanet research.

title: Student-powered 'moon' rovers put to the test | Space photo of the day for June 2, 2026
summary: Hundreds of students designed, built, and tested rovers on an Earth obstacle course as part of a NASA challenge to prepare for lunar missions. The initiative aims to engage students in space technology development.

title: Celestron Origin Intelligent Home Observatory Mark II smart telescope review
summary: The Celestron Origin Mark II smart telescope allows users to capture high-quality images of deep-sky objects quickly after setup. It is reviewed as an intelligent home observatory device.

title: Massive boom over northeastern US was a meteor explosion as powerful as 300 tons of TNT, NASA confirms
summary: A massive boom heard over the northeastern US on May 30 was confirmed by NASA to be a meteor explosion equivalent to 300 tons of TNT. Satellite footage corroborated the event.

title: Unlocking 'the true space age': Impulse Space raises $500 million to build out fleet of ultra-mobile spacecraft
summary: Impulse Space, founded by Tom Mueller, SpaceX's first employee, raised $500 million to develop ultra-mobile spacecraft aimed at transforming the space economy.

title: Will SpaceX still be a launch company after its historic IPO?
summary: SpaceX's upcoming IPO raises questions about its future as a launch company. The move could significantly impact investors, markets, and the broader space industry.

title: How do you study an invisible exoplanet? Astronomers discover planetary 'fingerprints' in the rings around stars
summary: Astronomers have discovered a method to study invisible exoplanets by analyzing their 'fingerprints' in the rings around stars. This technique allows them to infer the presence and even the mass of planets that cannot be directly observed.

title: Shockwaves from dying stars may sculpt 'cosmic wagon wheel' stellar nurseries, simulations reveal
summary: Simulations show that shockwaves from dying stars sculpt 'cosmic wagon wheel' structures in molecular clouds, influencing star formation in the Milky Way.

title: Look Up!
summary: ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot and NASA astronaut Jack Hathaway monitored the SpaceX Dragon cargo spacecraft docking to the International Space Station on May 17, 2026. The event occurred 259 miles above Earth.

title: NASA Testing Wastewater Treatment Facility for Future Moon Base
summary: NASA has sent a mobile wastewater treatment system to the University of North Dakota for testing under lunar-like conditions, aiming to support future Moon and Mars missions. Graduate students will evaluate the technology's performance in simulated environments.

title: Be a Clump Scout and Help Reveal Secrets of Stellar Nurseries
summary: A citizen science project invites volunteers to identify star-forming clumps in galaxy images to train machine learning models. This initiative aims to enhance astronomical research by combining human and AI efforts.

title: Spacewalking With Scott Wray, Artemis EVA Training Lead
summary: Scott Wray, Artemis EVA Training Lead, shares his lifelong passion for spacewalks, beginning with imaginative play as a child. His journey highlights the influence of early experiences on a career in space exploration.

title: Fire’s Footprint on Santa Rosa Island
summary: A wildland fire burned across one-third of Santa Rosa Island, the second largest of the Channel Islands, affecting grassland, coastal sage scrub, and chaparral. The fire's impact on the island's ecosystem is significant.

title: NASA Awards Modification Contract for Reduced Gravity Test Aircraft
summary: NASA awarded a modification contract to Denmar Technical Services for aircraft modifications and maintenance for reduced gravity testing. The contract covers services for NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center and Johnson Space Center.

title: NASA Invites Media to See Roman Space Telescope Arrive at Kennedy
summary: NASA invites media to cover the arrival of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope at Kennedy Space Center. The telescope will be transported via NASA's Pegasus barge from Goddard Space Flight Center.

title: NASA to Conduct Low-Altitude Flights Near Houston
summary: NASA will conduct low-altitude research flights near Houston from June 3 to June 13 as part of the Student Airborne Research Program (SARP). Five aircraft will fly remote sensing payloads in parallel patterns to collect data over the Houston area.

title: What’s Up: June 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for June 2026, including a Venus-Jupiter conjunction after sunset and the summer solstice. The Moon will also occult Venus during the month.

title: Pretty in Pink
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory and James Webb Space Telescope released a composite image of the star cluster Westerlund 2 on March 19, 2026, showcasing stars aged between one and three million years. The image combines X-ray data (pink) with infrared data (multicolored) to highlight the cluster's features.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are planned, with SpaceX and Chinese providers leading the manifest.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a major anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the debut flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo satellite launches dominating the manifest.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research on the ISS.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to the Chinese Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from Boca Chica, Texas, demonstrating significant performance improvements. This marks a key milestone in SpaceX's Starship development program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of space exploration and innovation.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative opens new pathways for advancing space missions and accessibility.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a parachute system for a space mission, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test is likely aimed at ensuring the parachute's reliability under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter diameter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid microbial contamination. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of past or present life on Mars.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling secure handling of objects in orbit. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Northrop Grumman Taps Apex for Golden Dome SBI Collab
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex to collaborate on the Golden Dome SBI mission, aiming to deliver on-orbit missile defense capabilities by 2027.

title: Voyager Will Acquire Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Space Holdings announced its acquisition of Astrobotic, positioning itself as a comprehensive lunar platform provider. This move aims to support NASA's Artemis program by offering infrastructure capabilities for lunar surface operations.

title: Impulse Space Closes $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has closed a $500 million Series D funding round, focusing on continuing its current trajectory rather than expanding into new ventures or facilities.

title: Bellatrix Aerospace Tapped to Build Korean VLEO Demo Sat
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace is partnering with Korean optical payload manufacturer TelePIX to build a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) demonstration satellite, targeting a launch no earlier than 2028.

title: Opinion: Commercial Space Is Generating Signals Decision-Makers Cannot Hear
summary: Commercial space activities are producing vast amounts of operational data, but decision-makers struggle to process these signals effectively. The current system fails to ensure critical information reaches those who need it.

title: Nuggets in the SpaceX S-1 and Our Thoughts on the Business
summary: SpaceX's IPO prospectus has been released, revealing key insights about the company's business. The document provides an overview of SpaceX's financial health and growth strategies.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a launch attempt on the pad. The incident occurred during a recent launch attempt.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe highlighted its progress in achieving orbital sovereignty at SmallSat Europe. The event emphasized the continent's advancements in space independence.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, raised $90 million in a Series A funding round to expand its production of laser and optical hardware and grow internationally beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with a new investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Are Orbital Data Centers the Next Frontier of AI Infrastructure?
summary: The space industry is exploring orbital data centers to support AI infrastructure, with competition among hyperscalers in cloud, AI, and space sectors. This trend is seen as a potential game-changer for future computing needs.

title: Sighting the Dark Fleet: Hyperspectral’s Role in Securing Safer Seas
summary: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation to U.S. and Israeli strikes, escalating a regional war. Hyperspectral technology is highlighted for securing maritime safety amid the conflict.

title: Orbital Data Centers: The Hype is There. Will the Reality Match it?
summary: The article discusses the hype surrounding orbital data centers in the satellite industry and questions whether the reality will live up to expectations. It highlights the industry's tendency to chase the next 'big thing' in space.

title: From Capacity to Capability: The New Playbook for Asia-Pacific Operators
summary: Regional satellite operators in the Asia-Pacific are shifting from traditional capacity-based models to capability-driven strategies due to the rise of megaconstellations and global consolidation. They are focusing on orchestration and integration to remain competitive.

title: How Can We Respond to the Skills Gap in Space?
summary: The 2023 Space Sector Skills Survey by the Space Skills Alliance reveals that 52% of U.K. space organizations face skills gaps in their workforce. The article discusses potential responses to this growing challenge in the space industry.

title: The Earth Observation Market and Regulation: Instruments for Growth
summary: Satellite Earth Observation (EO) is highlighted as a key tool for Europe's defense, green transition, and digital transformation. The article discusses its role in monitoring environmental issues like droughts and forest fires.

title: Mexican Airline Viva Taps SES to Connect 100 Aircraft
summary: Mexican low-cost airline Viva has partnered with SES to provide multi-orbit connectivity to 100 Airbus aircraft. SES will equip 60 Airbus A320s and 40 Airbus A321s with satellite connectivity.

title: Northrop Grumman to Team With Apex for Space-Based Interceptors, Targeting Capability in 2027
summary: Northrop Grumman announced a partnership with space startup Apex to develop space-based interceptors (SBIs) targeting an on-orbit capability by 2027. The collaboration aims to advance missile defense technology in space.

title: Viasat to Provide Satcom for Lockheed Martin-built ‘Hurricane Hunter’ Aircraft
summary: Viasat will equip two NOAA Hurricane Hunter aircraft with a hybrid satcom solution under a contract from Lockheed Martin. The Lockheed Martin-built aircraft are specialized planes used for hurricane tracking and research.

title: Polish Space Agency Exec Outlines Poland’s Growing Space Ambitions
summary: The Polish Space Agency, established in 2014, has significantly boosted Poland's commercial space sector, making it one of the seven largest contributors in the field.
